title: Lantana megapotamica
taxonomy: Lantana megapotamica is a species of flowering plant in the family Verbenaceae. This plant is native to South America and is also known as Purple Tangle or Purple Lantana. It belongs to the genus Lantana, which consists of about 150 species of shrubs and small trees. Lantana megapotamica is a perennial shrub that grows up to 3 meters tall, with clusters of small, fragrant flowers in shades of purple. The exact classification of Lantana megapotamica is Lantana megapotamica (L.) Britt.
uses: Lantana megapotamica, also known as the Panama pink shrub, is a medicinal plant with various uses. Its leaves and flowers have been used in traditional medicine to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The plant's antiseptic properties make it effective in treating wounds and skin infections. Additionally, Lantana megapotamica has been used to treat digestive problems and as an anti-inflammatory agent. Its medicinal uses are still being researched for their potential benefits in treating various health conditions.

constituents: Lantana megapotamica, also known as the common lantana or shrub verbena, contains a variety of active constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins. These compounds are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Research has also identified the presence of triterpenoids and diterpenes in Lantana megapotamica, which have been shown to have antimicrobial and antiviral properties. Overall, the diversity of active constituents in Lantana megapotamica makes it a valuable plant for traditional medicine and modern research applications.

side_effects: Lantana megapotamica can cause skin irritation, allergic reactions, and respiratory issues in some individuals. This is often due to the plant's high concentration of lantana alkaloids and other compounds that can be toxic. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap or pollen can exacerbate these symptoms. In rare cases, ingesting any part of the Lantana megapotamica plant can lead to more severe health complications, including organ damage. It is essential to handle the plant with caution and avoid consuming it altogether.
intro: Lantana megapotamica, also known as Purple Sage, is a flowering plant species native to South America, belonging to the Verbenaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the reduction of inflammation and the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Lantana megapotamica include lantadene, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Purple Sage include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to alleviate coughs and relieve respiratory discomfort.
